# AmericasOilWatch

> Western Hemisphere oil and fuel security intelligence — WTI crude price, US petroleum stocks, producer snapshots, and supply route risk from Canada to Patagonia.

AmericasOilWatch is an independent dashboard tracking oil production, prices, and supply route risk across the Western Hemisphere. It covers 12 producer countries and presents EIA data in a clear, accessible format for energy professionals, journalists, policymakers, and the public.

## Key Data

- WTI crude spot price (weekly, EIA/Yahoo Finance) — Cushing, Oklahoma benchmark
- US commercial crude oil stocks (weekly, EIA) — million barrels
- US gasoline and distillate stocks (weekly, EIA) — million barrels
- US Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) level
- US crude oil production — thousand barrels per day
- US retail gasoline and diesel prices (weekly, EIA) — USD per gallon
- Brent crude benchmark for international spread context
- Maritime advisory status from MARAD (US Maritime Administration)
- Energy research from CREA (Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air)
- AI-generated market analysis updated daily

## Countries Covered

**North America:** United States, Canada, Mexico
**Central America/Caribbean:** Trinidad & Tobago
**South America:** Brazil, Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Argentina, Guyana, Peru, Bolivia

## Key Chokepoints Tracked

- Panama Canal — drought risk, vessel restrictions
- Strait of Hormuz — currently CRITICAL (reclosed April 2026)
- Bab-el-Mandeb — currently CRITICAL (Houthi attacks ongoing)
- Straits of Florida — normal
- Gulf of Mexico — hurricane season monitoring
- Strait of Magellan / Drake Passage — alternative Cape route

## Current Situation (April 2026)

- WTI crude: ~$82/bbl
- Brent crude: ~$90/bbl (WTI-Brent spread ~-$8)
- US crude production: ~13,300 kb/d (world's largest producer)
- US commercial crude stocks: ~430 MB
- US gasoline: ~$4.25/gal national average
- Strait of Hormuz reclosed 18 April 2026 — unprecedented dual closure with Bab-el-Mandeb
- Dual closure driving Atlantic Basin producers (US shale, Brazil, Guyana) as swing suppliers
- Guyana Stabroek block fastest-growing new producer globally (~650 kb/d, targeting 1.2m by 2027)
- Argentina Vaca Muerta shale development accelerating

## Pages

- [Dashboard](https://americasoilwatch.com): WTI price, US stocks, AI analysis, producer grid
- [Supply Routes](https://americasoilwatch.com/supply): Chokepoint status, MARAD advisories, CREA research
- [Prices](https://americasoilwatch.com/prices): WTI benchmark, US retail prices, Americas price context
- [Analysis](https://americasoilwatch.com/analysis): Full AI-generated market analysis
- [Methodology](https://americasoilwatch.com/methodology): Data sources and methods
- [About](https://americasoilwatch.com/about): About the project

## Data Sources

- US Energy Information Administration (EIA): Weekly petroleum stocks, WTI price, retail prices
- Yahoo Finance (CL=F): WTI crude futures price (fallback)
- Yahoo Finance (BZ=F): Brent crude futures price
- US Maritime Administration (MARAD): Maritime security advisories
- Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA): Energy research RSS
- Ship & Bunker: Bunker fuel prices at Rotterdam, Fujairah, Singapore

## Related Sites

- [EuroOilWatch](https://eurooilwatch.com): EU fuel reserves and prices across 27 countries
- [UKOilWatch](https://ukoilwatch.com): UK fuel reserves and prices
